date_discipline: All evidence dated on or before 2026-04-28. In-window = 2026-04-14 through 2026-04-28; older context informs but does not anchor proposals. No post-2026-04-28 events used in scoring.
deterministic_baseline_source: site/src/data/indexes/*.json (per assessor instruction). Drift from rotation-state.json and user-provided baselines noted in individual proposals where relevant (Microsoft 76.8 user input vs 66.4 live; Masimo 76.4 rotation vs 48.4 live).

errors.name: Oracle
errors.reason: Entity not present in live site/src/data/indexes/fortune-500.json. Cannot retrieve published baseline. Scanner-flagged March 31 30K layoff event documented but no entity record exists to anchor proposal against. Recommend index reconciliation pass to add Oracle to Fortune 500 entity list.

results.key_finding: SIXTH consecutive deferral. UNMISS mandate expires April 30 (T-2 days). What's In Blue and press.un.org confirm no scheduled or held renewal vote in publicly accessible primary sources as of April 28. 2025 cycle precedent: technical rollover (Res 2778) on April 30 then substantive Res 2779 May 8. Hard-deadline protocol activated; escalated to PENDING_CHANGES.md human-review queue.

Composite 2.2 holds at floor. April 20 Yaccarino+Musk French summons, Amsterdam injunction, EU Parliament deepfake ban already in prior baseline. No new acute April 25-28 evidence. Floor-limitation methodology gap now overdue 6+ nights for this entity.

Trial Day 2 opening arguments delivered April 28. Two surviving claims (unjust enrichment, breach of charitable trust); $134B sought; Altman/Brockman removal sought. Judge Gonzalez Rogers makes remedy decision; jury advisory only. Composite 27.5 (Developing) appropriately reflects unresolved governance controversy. Post-trial re-queue mandatory after May 21 liability phase + remedy decision.

Composite 6.6 holds (rebaselined yesterday from 10.3 calibration proposal). April 24 Intercept IRS scope expansion already absorbed. UK Parliament April 16 debate, 32-officials revolving-door, 13-employee whistleblower letter all in prior captures. Floor-limitation methodology gap continues.

April 22 96-page DC Circuit appeals filing is substantive positive INT/ACC signal — Anthropic explicitly defends Claude classified-network non-manipulability AND maintains refusal to permit mass domestic surveillance + autonomous weapons use. Parallel SF federal court win removed stigmatizing labels. SB3444 opposition (vs OpenAI support) further supports INT positioning. Composite 61.6 (Established, +1.6 above 60-band boundary) holds.

April 23 8K layoffs + 6K cancelled open roles announced. NM jury ($375M potential) and LA jury ($6M with Google) verdicts confirm rather than newly add to BND/INT. Sub-5pt EMP calibration available but not warranting proposal (entity is mid-Developing, expressive room exists upward and downward). Composite 29.4 holds.

Composite 0 holds at floor. New in-window: Al Jazeera April 27 Sudan Doctors Network reports 1,470+ civilians + 907 military + 20 doctors held in dire RSF detention with cholera outbreak; ethnic-targeted torture/execution. UN Security Council April 28 added new Resolution 1591 sanctions designations. Genocide finding remains institutional anchor. Floor-limitation methodology gap continues.

April 23 first-ever voluntary buyout in 51-year history: ~7% US workforce / 8,750 employees, Rule of 70 eligibility, voluntary opt-in. Materially less coercive than Meta/Oracle direct layoffs but TheNextWeb characterization as 'buyout dressed as a benefit' applies. Sub-5pt EMP calibration available; composite 66.4 holds. DATA INTEGRITY FLAG: User input claimed 76.8 baseline; live data shows 66.4 — reconciliation needed.

April 17 Frontier Safety Framework v3 published — adds Tracked Capability Levels, new Critical Capability Level for harmful manipulation, extends misalignment protocols to large-scale internal deployments. Positive INT/SYS signal pre-EU AI Act enforcement (96 days). Lacks specified independent external oversight (limits ACC upgrade). Composite 65.0 holds. First substantive evidence-based confirmation for entity (rotation_state.last_assessed = null prior).

Composite 8.8 holds at near-floor. OCHA April 23 Humanitarian Situation Report: dire conditions continue but aid entry surged 14-20 April with Zikim Crossing reopening (partial mitigation). 37 NGO suspension + Israeli High Court injunction still in litigation. April 28 UN Security Council Gaza ceasefire briefing held. Amnesty 2026 quasi-judicial finding remains institutional anchor.

California AG Bonta April 20 unsealed evidence in pending antitrust case — Amazon directed brands and rival retailers to inflate prices on competing platforms. Trial 2027. Composite 17.8 (Critical) already deeply reflects accumulated evidence. Sub-5pt INT calibration available; not warranting proposal.
ENTITY NOT IN LIVE INDEX. Cannot anchor March 31 30K-layoff evidence to a published baseline. Recommend index reconciliation pass to add Oracle.
Treasury cancellation of all 31 contracts ($21M) following IRS contractor unauthorized-disclosure conviction already absorbed in April 25 baseline (composite 36.7). No new April 25-28 evidence.

Medicare GLP-1 access agreement (Zepbound $50/month cap; orforglipron pending; Medicaid expansion; ~40M Americans benefit) effective April 1 already absorbed in April 25 baseline. Foundayo (orforglipron) FDA approval supports ACT. Composite 56.3 (Functional) holds.
Composite 10.9 (Critical) already deeply reflects accumulated evidence. April 23 8K layoffs + child-safety AI scandal + jury verdicts confirm rather than newly add.

Tier 1 LGBTQ rights globally; same-sex marriage 2013; anti-discrimination 2004. Transgender life expectancy ~45 years and physical violence ⅔ rates represent EQU ceiling. Composite 83 (Exemplary) holds.

Strong civil liberties (Freedom House 2026); same-sex marriage entered force January 2026; GRECO May 2025 noted gaps re prince's prosecutorial-block authority; UN expert flagged financial-secrecy-as-rights-harm; 2026 GIR MCAA tax-cooperation. Composite 83 holds.
100% renewable grid; Comprehensive Migration Policy 2024-2034 protects climate-displaced; State Dept: no significant rights changes. Composite 81.4 (Exemplary) holds.

Patient-safety mission (Patient SafetyNet); Sustainalytics ESG 'Average'; February 2026 Danaher acquisition agreement ($9.9B) pending. Composite 48.4 (post-correction baseline) holds. Re-queue mandatory at acquisition close.

9 consecutive years 100% on HRC Corporate Equality Index; Forbes Best Employers for Diversity 2018-2023; 5 perfect Disability Equality Index scores; pension plan offered (rare); year 10 of 15-year diversity roadmap. Composite 77.9 (Established) holds.

methodology_flags.description: SIXTH CONSECUTIVE DEFERRAL. UNMISS mandate expires April 30 (T-2 days). Vote not retrievable in public sources. Hard-deadline protocol activated; escalated to PENDING_CHANGES.md human-review queue. CANNOT BE DEFERRED FURTHER.

Five entities at or near floor (xAI 2.2, Palantir 6.6, Sudan 0, South Sudan 0, Israel 8.8) with accumulating new evidence each night. Methodology gap now overdue 7 nights cumulatively. Recommend dedicated methodology development cycle: (a) permanent-floor designation framework, (b) cumulative-evidence-tier weighting, or (c) sub-score for harm-scale beyond 0-100 dimensional scale.

User-provided baseline for Microsoft (76.8) does not match live site/data (66.4). Scanner rotation_state for Masimo (76.4) does not match live site/data (48.4). Used live data per assessor instruction. Recommend rotation-state and user-input reconciliation pass before next batch apply.

Oracle flagged as priority T2 by scanner but not present in live fortune-500 index. Cannot generate proposal without baseline. Recommend index reconciliation to add Oracle (March 31 30K-layoff event is material).

south_sudan_resolution: DEFERRED (6th consecutive). Direct retrieval attempted via Security Council Report What's In Blue (no UNMISS renewal entry), press.un.org April 25-28 releases (covers Resolution 1988, Sudan sanctions Res 1591, Hormuz, Haiti, Gaza ceasefire — no UNMISS), and UNMISS dedicated search (only 2025 cycle results). 2025 cycle precedent: technical rollover Res 2778 on April 30, then Res 2779 May 8. Same delay pattern plausible. Hard-deadline protocol activated; escalated to PENDING_CHANGES.md human-review queue with explicit April 30 marker. Floor-limitation means any vote outcome cannot be expressed via composite movement; methodology-note registration is the appropriate response.
key_findings: Zero standard proposals generated tonight. Today's window did not produce evidence crossing the 5-point composite threshold for any entity at the live baseline.
Five floor-limited confirmations (xAI, Palantir, Sudan, Israel, plus South Sudan deferral) document continuing accumulation of evidence the methodology cannot express.

April 17 DeepMind Frontier Safety Framework v3 is the night's most significant POSITIVE substantive event (Tracked Capability Levels, harmful-manipulation CCL, internal-deployment safety case requirements) — confirms composite 65.0.

April 22 Anthropic 96-page DC Circuit filing is the night's most significant POSITIVE INT signal — explicit defense of Claude classified-network non-manipulability + maintained refusal of mass-surveillance/autonomous-weapons use.
April 28 Musk v. Altman trial Day 2 opening arguments are deterministic procedural events — substantive verdict still ~3 weeks out; OpenAI confirm appropriate.
Microsoft buyout (April 23) is voluntary mechanism qualitatively distinct from Meta/Oracle direct layoffs — methodology should consider voluntariness as EMP modifier in future iterations.
Oracle flagged as priority but not in live Fortune 500 index — index reconciliation needed.
User-provided Microsoft baseline (76.8) and rotation_state Masimo baseline (76.4) both diverge from live data (66.4 and 48.4 respectively) — input reconciliation recommended.
